title: pithecellobium fallax
intro: Pithecellobium fallax, also known as the False Parkinsonia, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pithecellobium fallax include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by boiling the leaves and bark in water, and are often consumed as teas or infusions.
